Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

This patch introduced a regression failure in sanity test 24D. I'm investigating.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Andreas,

With this path applied, sanity test 24D failed as follows:

  test  0: Read/write layout attributes then create a file ................................. pass
 llapi_layout_test: llapi_layout_test.c:164: __test1_helper: assertion 'ost0 == T0_OST_OFFSET' failed: 16777216 != 1
  test  1: Read test0 file by path and verify attributes ................................... fail (exit status 1)
 llapi_layout_test: llapi_layout_test.c:164: __test1_helper: assertion 'ost0 == T0_OST_OFFSET' failed: 16777216 != 1
  test  2: Read test0 file by FD and verify attributes ..................................... fail (exit status 1)
 llapi_layout_test: llapi_layout_test.c:164: __test1_helper: assertion 'ost0 == T0_OST_OFFSET' failed: 16777216 != 1
  test  3: Read test0 file by FID and verify attributes .................................... fail (exit status 1)
 <~snip~>
 llapi_layout_test: llapi_layout_test.c:1266: test29: assertion 'ost0 == 1' failed: 16777216 != 1
  test 29: set ost index to non-zero stripe number ......................................... fail (exit status 1)


This is because while lmm_magic is LOV_USER_MAGIC_V1 or LOV_USER_MAGIC_V3, l_ost_idx is not swabbed in lustre_swab_lov_user_md(), which is called by ll_getxattr_lov().

Without this patch, the above tests passed because the l_ost_idx was swabbed in llapi_layout_swab_lov_user_md(), which was called by llapi_layout_get_by_xattr() after running fgetxattr() in llapi_layout_get_by_fd().

To resolve this issue, I'm not sure if we should add lustre_swab_lov_user_md_objects() for LOV_USER_MAGIC_V1 and LOV_USER_MAGIC_V3 in lustre_swab_lov_user_md(), and remove llapi_layout_swab_lov_user_md().

Could you please advise?

Patch Set 1:

> With this patch applied, sanity test 24D failed as follows:
[snip]
 > This is because while lmm_magic is LOV_USER_MAGIC_V1 or
 > LOV_USER_MAGIC_V3, l_ost_idx is not swabbed in lustre_swab_lov_user_md(),
 > which is called by ll_getxattr_lov().
 > 
 > Without this patch, the above tests passed because the l_ost_idx
 > was swabbed in llapi_layout_swab_lov_user_md(), which was called
 > by llapi_layout_get_by_xattr() after running fgetxattr() in
 > llapi_layout_get_by_fd().
 > 
 > To resolve this issue, I'm not sure if we should add
 > lustre_swab_lov_user_md_objects() for LOV_USER_MAGIC_V1 and
 > LOV_USER_MAGIC_V3 in lustre_swab_lov_user_md(),

Yes, it definitely does not make sense to swab only the header and not the objects.  The objects should be swabbed as well, *IFF* they are present and this isn't just a directory default layout.

> and remove llapi_layout_swab_lov_user_md().

No, it still makes sense to do both:
- not all applications are calling llapi_layout_* functions
  (e.g. MPICH), so swabbing to host-endian in the kernel is good
- applications may get the xattr from various sources (e.g. tar
  archive, from ldiskfs, etc. via llapi_layout_get_by_xattr()),
  so there still may need to be swabbing done in userspace

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Mark -1 until objects are swabbed.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-11873 tests: Increase barrier freeze time

commit.subject: LU-12589 llite: swab LOV EA data in ll_getxattr_lov()
commit.message:
LU-12589 llite: swab LOV EA data in ll_getxattr_lov()

On PPC client, the LOV EA data returned by getfattr from x86_64 server
was not swabbed to the host endian. While running setfattr, the data was
swabbed in ll_lov_setstripe_ea_info(), which caused magic mis-match in
ll_lov_user_md_size() and then ll_setstripe_ea() returned -ERANGE.

This patch fixed the above issue by swabbing LOV EA data in ll_getxattr_lov().

Test-Parameters: clientarch=ppc64 \
envdefinitions=ONLY="24D 102a" testlist=sanity

Change-Id: I8069df0c8f07c0bedba2e27db7c3a5553f11afb4
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35626
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
